vulnerability.reference: LoRA adapters fine-tuned on narrow task data can overwrite safety-critical weights, reducing refusal robustness.
vulnerability.generated: Safety-critical regions are sparse, entangled with utility weights, and lack isolation mechanisms, causing accidental removal during low-rank adaptation.

impact.reference: Domain-adapted models in high-stakes fields (e.g., medicine, law) become capable of generating targeted harmful advice, eroding professional trust.
impact.generated: Adapted models become jailbroken, emitting harmful outputs that directly harm end-users and erode trust in safety-aligned AI systems.

dose_response.reference: Brittle safety alignment enables harmful outputs, which in low-rank adaptation by practitioners overwrites safety-critical weights, leading to targeted harmful advice and lost professional trust.
dose_response.generated: The brittleness of safety alignment to low-rank weight modifications, through LoRA domain adaptation, triggers accidental removal of sparse, entangled safety-critical regions, leading to jailbroken models emitting harmful outputs and eroding trust.
